tEn: Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know
tZh: 语言模型（大多）知道它们知道什么
sEn: Language models can self-evaluate the validity of their own claims and predict answerability, with performance improving through self-consistency and context.
sZh: 语言模型能够自我评估自身主张的有效性并预测可回答性，通过自洽性和上下文信息可提升表现。
absEn: We study whether language models can evaluate the validity of their own claims and predict which questions they will be able to answer correctly. We first show that larger models are well-calibrated on diverse multiple choice and true/false questions when they are provided in the right format. Thus we can approach self-evaluation on open-ended sampling tasks by asking models to first propose answers, and then to evaluate the probability "P(True)" that their answers are correct. We find encouraging performance, calibration, and scaling for P(True) on a diverse array of tasks. Performance at self-evaluation further improves when we allow models to consider many of their own samples before predicting the validity of one specific possibility. Next, we investigate whether models can be trained to predict "P(IK)", the probability that "I know" the answer to a question, without reference to any particular proposed answer. Models perform well at predicting P(IK) and partially generalize across tasks, though they struggle with calibration of P(IK) on new tasks. The predicted P(IK) probabilities also increase appropriately in the presence of relevant source materials in the context, and in the presence of hints towards the solution of mathematical word problems. We hope these observations lay the groundwork for training more honest models, and for investigating how honesty generalizes to cases where models are trained on objectives other than the imitation of human writing.
absZh: 我们研究了语言模型是否能评估自身主张的有效性，并预测哪些问题能正确回答。首先，我们发现，当以正确格式提供时，较大的模型在多样化的多项选择和真假问题上校准良好。因此，我们可以通过让模型先提出答案，然后评估其答案正确的概率“P(True)”来接近开放采样任务上的自我评估。我们发现，在多样化的任务上，P(True)在性能、校准和扩展性方面表现令人鼓舞。当允许模型在预测某一特定可能性的有效性之前考虑自己的多个样本时，自我评估的性能进一步提升。接下来，我们研究了模型是否可以被训练来预测“P(IK)”，即“我知道”问题答案的概率，而不参考任何特定提出的答案。模型在预测P(IK)方面表现良好，并在任务间部分泛化，尽管在新任务上P(IK)的校准存在困难。预测的P(IK)概率在上下文中存在相关源材料时，以及在数学文字题提示解决方案时，也会适当增加。我们希望这些观察为训练更诚实的模型奠定基础，并研究当模型在模仿人类写作之外的目标上训练时，诚实如何泛化。

contrib.en: Larger language models are well-calibrated on diverse multiple-choice and true/false questions when formatted appropriately.
contrib.zh: 较大的语言模型在适当格式化的多种选择题和是非题上校准良好。

contrib.en: Self-evaluation via P(True) shows encouraging performance, calibration, and scaling across tasks.
contrib.zh: 通过P(True)进行自我评估在各项任务上表现出令人鼓舞的性能、校准和规模扩展。

contrib.en: Providing models with many of their own samples improves self-evaluation performance.
contrib.zh: 向模型提供其自身的多个样本可提升自我评估性能。

contrib.en: Models can be trained to predict P(IK) and partially generalize across tasks, though calibration on new tasks is challenging.
contrib.zh: 模型可被训练预测P(IK)并在任务间部分泛化，但新任务上的校准具有挑战性。

contrib.en: P(IK) increases appropriately when relevant source materials or hints are provided in context.
contrib.zh: 当上下文中提供相关源材料或提示时，P(IK)会适当增加。

contrib.en: Self-evaluation and calibration improve with model size, suggesting verification scales faster than generation.
contrib.zh: 自我评估和校准随模型规模提升，表明验证比生成扩展更快。

limits.en: Study focuses on pretrained language models; RLHF policies require temperature adjustment for calibration.
limits.zh: 研究聚焦于预训练语言模型；RLHF策略需温度调整以实现校准。

limits.en: P(IK) generalization is partial and calibration suffers out-of-distribution.
limits.zh: P(IK)泛化不完全，且校准在分布外表现不佳。

limits.en: Self-evaluation is zero-shot poorly calibrated; few-shot prompting is needed for good calibration.
limits.zh: 零样本自我评估校准不佳；需少样本提示才能获得良好校准。

limits.en: Work does not address intentional deception or distinguish truth from human consensus.
limits.zh: 工作未涉及有意欺骗，也未区分真理与人类共识。

limits.en: Only five sampling-based datasets were studied, limiting scope of generalization observations.
limits.zh: 仅研究了五个基于采样的数据集，限制了泛化观察的范围。

full.sec: Abstract

items.en: We study whether language models can evaluate the validity of their own claims and predict which questions they will be able to answer correctly. We first show that larger models are well-calibrated on diverse multiple choice and true/false questions when they are provided in the right format. Thus we can approach self-evaluation on open-ended sampling tasks by asking models to first propose answers, and then to evaluate the probability "P(True)" that their answers are correct. We find encouraging performance, calibration, and scaling for P(True) on a diverse array of tasks. Performance at self-evaluation further improves when we allow models to consider many of their own samples before predicting the validity of one specific possibility. Next, we investigate whether models can be trained to predict "P(IK)", the probability that "I know" the answer to a question, without reference to any particular proposed answer. Models perform well at predicting P(IK) and partially generalize across tasks, though they struggle with calibration of P(IK) on new tasks. The predicted P(IK) probabilities also increase appropriately in the presence of relevant source materials in the context, and in the presence of hints towards the solution of mathematical word problems. We hope these observations lay the groundwork for training more honest models, and for investigating how honesty generalizes to cases where models are trained on objectives other than the imitation of human writing.
items.zh: 我们研究语言模型能否评估自身主张的有效性，并预测哪些问题能正确回答。我们首先表明，当以正确格式提供时，较大模型在多种选择题和是非题上校准良好。因此，我们可以通过让模型先提出答案，然后评估其答案正确的概率“P(True)”来处理开放式采样任务上的自我评估。我们在多种任务上观察到P(True)令人鼓舞的性能、校准和缩放。当允许模型在预测某个特定可能性的有效性之前考虑自身的多个样本时，自我评估的性能进一步提升。接下来，我们研究模型能否被训练来预测“P(IK)”，即“我知道”问题答案的概率，而不参考任何特定提出的答案。模型在预测P(IK)上表现良好，并在任务间部分泛化，尽管在新任务上P(IK)的校准存在困难。预测的P(IK)概率在上下文中存在相关源材料时，以及在数学文字题提示存在时，也会适当增加。我们希望这些观察为训练更诚实的模型，以及研究诚实性如何泛化到模型训练目标并非模仿人类写作的情况奠定基础。

full.sec: 1 Introduction

items.en: We would eventually like to train AI systems that are honest, which requires that these systems accurately and faithfully evaluate their level of confidence in their own knowledge and reasoning. So AI systems must be able to recognize what they do and do not know, as a prerequisite. In this work, we study the extent to which Language Models (LMs) possess this ability and how it can be elicited and imparted.
items.zh: 我们最终希望训练诚实的AI系统，这要求这些系统准确且忠实地评估自身知识和推理的置信水平。因此，AI系统必须能够识别自身知道和不知道的内容，这是前提。在这项工作中，我们研究语言模型（LM）拥有这种能力的程度，以及如何激发和赋予这种能力。

items.en: As a starting point, we examine calibration: do the probabilistic predictions from language models match up with frequencies of occurrence? Language models can produce well-calibrated predictions for token probabilities on-distribution [Guo et al., 2017]. We show that large language models are also well-calibrated on a diverse array of multiple choice questions, as long as the questions are formatted appropriately. In particular, calibration improves with model size and few-shot prompting.
items.zh: 作为起点，我们考察校准：语言模型的概率预测是否与发生频率匹配？语言模型可以在分布内对词元概率产生良好校准的预测[Guo et al., 2017]。我们表明，只要问题格式适当，大型语言模型在多种选择题上也校准良好。特别是，校准随模型规模和少样本提示而改善。

items.capEn: Figure 1: (left) We show the overall ability of a 52B language model to evaluate its own proposed answers (sampled at unit temperature) to questions from TriviaQA, Lambada, Arithmetic, GSM8k, and Codex HumanEval. We have weighted the overall contribution from each of these five datasets equally. We evaluate 20-shot using the method of section 4, where we show the model several of its own samples and then ask for the probability P(True) that a specific sample is correct. (right) We show the improvement in the accuracy on each sampling task when only including questions where a randomly sampled (unit temperature) response achieved P(True) > 0.5.
items.capZh: 图1：（左）我们展示了一个52B语言模型评估自身对TriviaQA、Lambada、算术、GSM8k和Codex HumanEval问题提出的答案（在单位温度下采样）的整体能力。我们等权平均了这五个数据集的总体贡献。我们使用第4节的方法进行20次评估，其中我们向模型展示其自身的几个样本，然后询问特定样本正确的概率P(True)。（右）我们展示了当仅包含随机采样（单位温度）响应达到P(True) > 0.5的问题时，每个采样任务准确率的提升。

items.en: Good calibration opens up the possibility for using models to evaluate the accuracy of their own outputs (“self-evaluation”). For example, given any open-ended query, we can sample an answer from the model and then have the model evaluate P(True), the probability that its answer is correct. We may expect self-evaluation to be challenging, because the model may be overconfident that its own samples111Another more subtle issue is that in our setup, models have no way to distinguish the tokens they’ve written from tokens they were given by a third party. are correct. Our self-evaluation procedure nevertheless distinguishes correct and incorrect samples, as summarized in Figure 1. Furthermore, as model size and capabilities increase, models improve at self-evaluation, which suggests that verification improves faster than generation quality in this context.
items.zh: 良好的校准为使用模型评估自身输出的准确性（“自我评估”）开辟了可能性。例如，对于任何开放式查询，我们可以从模型采样一个答案，然后让模型评估P(True)，即其答案正确的概率。我们可能预期自我评估具有挑战性，因为模型可能对其自身样本过于自信。然而，我们的自我评估程序仍然区分了正确和错误的样本，如图1总结所示。此外，随着模型规模和能力的增加，模型在自我评估上有所改进，这表明在此背景下验证比生成质量提升更快。

items.en: We also show that self-evaluation can be improved if we provide a model with many of its own samples, before asking it to evaluate any single sample. That is, ‘brainstorming other possibilities’ helps large models to evaluate the validity of a given answer option.
items.zh: 我们还表明，如果在要求模型评估任何单个样本之前，向其提供自身的多个样本，自我评估可以得到改善。也就是说，“头脑风暴其他可能性”有助于大型模型评估给定答案选项的有效性。

items.en: These techniques address a question about the world, as they ask models to evaluate “according to accepted truth in the wider world (i.e. according to humans), is a particular answer to a question correct?” In the case of self-evaluation, the proposed answer was provided by the model, but its validity is nevertheless an external fact.
items.zh: 这些技术处理关于世界的问题，因为它们要求模型评估“根据更广泛世界中公认的真理（即根据人类），某个问题的特定答案是否正确？”在自我评估的情况下，提出的答案由模型提供，但其有效性仍然是外部事实。

items.en: But we are also interested in having language models attempt to directly evaluate their own state of knowledge. To this purpose, we investigate what happens when we train models to predict whether or not they can correctly answer questions themselves. This is really a question about the model222This claim is subtle, since the model could be learning “how hard is this question in general?” rather than “do I know the answer to this?” We partially address this concern in section 5.5. since we are training the model to learn what sorts of questions it, in particular, can answer.
items.zh: 但我们也对让语言模型直接评估自身知识状态感兴趣。为此，我们研究训练模型预测它们是否能正确回答问题本身时会发生什么。这实际上是一个关于模型的问题，因为我们训练模型了解它自己特别能回答哪些类型的问题。

items.en: We find that language models can easily learn to perform well at evaluating P(IK), the probability that they know the answer to a question, on a given distribution (see Figure 3 for an illustration). More intriguingly, we also find some generalization across tasks, for example from trivia to story completion, math, and code, though models struggle with calibration OOD. We also observe some other types of generalization: although P(IK) was only trained on bare questions, it generalizes in such a way that it increases when we provide source materials that address these questions (for trivia) and when we provide hints for math word problems.
items.zh: 我们发现语言模型可以轻松学会在给定分布上良好地评估P(IK)，即它们知道问题答案的概率（见图3示例）。更有趣的是，我们还观察到一些跨任务的泛化，例如从常识到故事完成、数学和代码，尽管模型在分布外校准上存在困难。我们还观察到其他类型的泛化：尽管P(IK)仅在裸问题上训练，但它以这样的方式泛化：当我们提供解决这些问题的源材料（对于常识）以及为数学文字题提供提示时，P(IK)会增加。

full.sec: 1.1 Contributions

items.en: We show that when we use a format with visible lettered answer options, large models are very well calibrated on diverse multiple choice questions (e.g. from BIG Bench [Srivastava et al., 2022], MMLU [Hendrycks et al., 2021], and many other evaluations); see Figures 4, 5, and 6.
items.zh: 我们表明，当使用带有可见字母选项的格式时，大型模型在多种选择题上校准非常好（例如来自BIG Bench [Srivastava et al., 2022]、MMLU [Hendrycks et al., 2021]和许多其他评估）；见图4、5和6。

items.en: Calibration improves with model size, and it also improves when we pass from zero-shot to few-shot evaluation; see Figure 4.
items.zh: 校准随模型规模而改善，并且从零样本评估过渡到少样本评估时也有所改善；见图4。

items.en: Replacing an option with ‘none of the above’ reduces accuracy and calibration significantly with our models (see Figure 7). However, our models are also well calibrated on True/False distinctions (see Figure 8), with accuracy and calibration also increasing with model capability.
items.zh: 在我们的模型中，将选项替换为“以上都不是”会显著降低准确率和校准（见图7）。然而，我们的模型在是非区分上也校准良好（见图8），准确率和校准也随模型能力增加而提高。

items.en: We also show that RLHF policies [Bai et al., 2022] naively seem miscalibrated, but with a simple temperature adjustment they become fairly well-calibrated on several evaluations (Figure 9).
items.zh: 我们还表明，RLHF策略[Bai et al., 2022]在表面上似乎校准不佳，但通过简单的温度调整，它们在几个评估上变得相当校准良好（图9）。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Left: We train a P(IK) classifier to predict whether or not a model knows the answer to TriviaQA questions, and then evaluate on Arithmetic, Python Function Synthesis, and Lambada questions. This histogram shows P(IK) scores exclusively from OOD questions. Right: We train a P(IK) classifier on TriviaQA, Arithmetic, Python Function Synthesis, and Lambada and histogram P(IK) scores for their test sets.
items.capZh: 图2：左：我们训练一个P(IK)分类器来预测模型是否知道TriviaQA问题的答案，然后在算术、Python函数合成和Lambada问题上进行评估。此直方图显示仅来自OOD问题的P(IK)分数。右：我们在TriviaQA、算术、Python函数合成和Lambada上训练一个P(IK)分类器，并对其测试集绘制P(IK)分数直方图。

items.en: We evaluate on the generative tasks TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017], Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016], GSM8k [Cobbe et al., 2021], the Codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021], arithmetic problems, and natural function synthesis problems scraped from GitHub. See Figure 1 for a brief overview of results.
items.zh: 我们在生成任务TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017]、Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016]、GSM8k [Cobbe et al., 2021]、Codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021]、算术问题和从GitHub抓取的自然函数合成问题上进行评估。结果简要概述见图1。

items.en: Models can self-evaluate whether their own samples are True or False, though this tends to be a more challenging task (since models tend to find their own samples more plausible). Self-evaluations are well-calibrated few-shot, though models aren’t as well-calibrated zero-shot. In particular, larger k𝑘k for k𝑘k-shot self-evaluation seems to primarily help by improving calibration, rather than by improving the AUROC for separating correct and incorrect responses. See Figures 10 and 11 in the main text for representative results, and Figures 28, 29, and 30 in the appendix for complete results.
items.zh: 模型可以自我评估其自身样本是真还是假，尽管这往往是一个更具挑战性的任务（因为模型倾向于认为自己的样本更合理）。自我评估在少样本下校准良好，尽管模型在零样本下校准不佳。特别是，对于k次自我评估，较大的k似乎主要通过改善校准来帮助，而不是通过改善区分正确和错误响应的AUROC。代表性结果见正文图10和11，完整结果见附录图28、29和30。

items.en: Showing models many of their own T=1𝑇1T=1 samples, along with a single sample to evaluate as True/False, can significantly improve their performance (this is somewhat reminiscent of self-consistency prompting [Wang et al., 2022]).
items.zh: 向模型展示其自身的多个T=1样本，以及一个待评估为真/假的单个样本，可以显著提高其性能（这有点类似于自一致性提示[Wang et al., 2022]）。

items.en: We conclude that language models can perform well at this task few-shot, with most measures of performance improving with model size, even though models are being asked to evaluate their own samples.
items.zh: 我们得出结论，语言模型可以在少样本下很好地完成此任务，大多数性能指标随模型规模而改善，尽管模型被要求评估自身的样本。

items.en: We are also interested in whether models know, or can be taught, to specifically identify questions that they can and cannot answer, rather than simply evaluating whether answers to questions are in fact correct. Figure 2 provides a brief overview of results.
items.zh: 我们还对模型是否知道或可以被教导专门识别它们能回答和不能回答的问题感兴趣，而不是简单地评估问题的答案是否正确。图2提供了结果的简要概述。

items.en: We train models with a value head to predict the probability that they can answer a question correctly, which we refer to as P(IK). We find that models trained on TriviaQA have significant power to differentiate between math, lambada, and code questions that they can answer; see Figure 12. However, P(IK) tends to be poorly calibrated on these other distributions (see Figure 13). Generalization is perhaps most clearly illustrated in Figures 16 and 17.
items.zh: 我们训练带有价值头的模型来预测它们能正确回答问题的概率，我们称之为P(IK)。我们发现，在TriviaQA上训练的模型具有显著能力来区分它们能回答的数学、Lambada和代码问题；见图12。然而，P(IK)在这些其他分布上往往校准不佳（见图13）。泛化可能最清楚地体现在图16和17中。

items.en: We study generalization of P(IK) to the inclusion of source materials and mathematical hints, see Figures 18 and 19. We find that P(IK) responds appropriately to sources, correct hints, and incorrect or distracting hints.
items.zh: 我们研究了 P(IK) 对包含源材料和数学提示的泛化情况，见图 18 和图 19。我们发现 P(IK) 能适当地响应源材料、正确提示以及错误或干扰性提示。

items.en: We compare two models with roughly equal capability, but different pretraining data, in Figure 20. These models are somewhat better at predicting their own P(IK) rather than that for another model. We also try "crossing" the P(IK) training data for these models, and find a small effect suggesting that models generalize better when trained on what they know, rather than on what the other model knew.
items.zh: 我们在图 20 中比较了两个能力大致相当但预训练数据不同的模型。这些模型在预测自身的 P(IK) 方面比预测另一个模型的 P(IK) 更好。我们还尝试了这些模型的 P(IK) 训练数据的“交叉”，发现一个微小效应，表明模型在基于自身所知而非另一个模型所知进行训练时泛化更好。

items.en: Thus we conclude that we only find partial generalization on this task.
items.zh: 因此我们得出结论：在此任务上仅发现部分泛化。

full.sec: Glossary: Observables and Metrics
full.secZh: 术语表：可观测值与度量

items.en: P(True) – The probability a model assigns to the proposition that a specific sample is the correct answer to a question.
items.zh: P(True)——模型分配给某个特定样本是问题正确答案这一命题的概率。

items.en: P(IK) – The probability a model assigns to "I know", i.e. the proposition that it will answer a given question correctly when samples are generated at unit temperature. In this work, P(IK) is usually computed using a binary classification head on top of a language model.
items.zh: P(IK)——模型分配给“我知道”的概率，即模型在单位温度下生成样本时能正确回答给定问题的命题。在本文中，P(IK) 通常通过语言模型顶部的二分类头计算。

items.en: Ground Truth P(IK) – The fraction of unit temperature samples to a question that are correct.
items.zh: 真实 P(IK)——针对一个问题，单位温度下正确样本的比例。

items.en: Calibration Charts - We often plot prediction probability vs frequency that a prediction was correct, see Figure 4 as an example. We use all predictions (not just predictions for the correct answer) and put the same number of predictions in each bin (rather than using equally spaced bins).
items.zh: 校准图——我们通常绘制预测概率与预测正确频率的关系图，例如图 4。我们使用所有预测（而不仅仅是正确答案的预测），并将相同数量的预测放入每个箱中（而不是使用等距箱）。

items.en: Expected or RMS Calibration Error - To obtain a single number summarizing calibration, we compute the ECE as the mean of the absolute difference between probabilistic predictions and frequencies. For ECE we always use 10 bins, with an equal number of predictions in each. What we call the ECE has also been called the mean absolute deviation calibration error [Lin et al., 2022]. To more closely match other works, we only use the most likely predictions (for multiple choice) when computing the ECE, instead of using all predictions. We also include the RMS calibration error, which is better motivated theoretically, but seems to be less widely used in the literature.
items.zh: 期望或均方根校准误差——为获得一个总结校准的单一数值，我们计算 ECE 作为概率预测与频率之间绝对差值的均值。对于 ECE，我们始终使用 10 个箱，每个箱中预测数量相等。我们所说的 ECE 也被称为平均绝对偏差校准误差 [Lin et al., 2022]。为与其他工作更一致，我们在计算 ECE 时仅使用最可能的预测（对于多项选择），而不是所有预测。我们还包含了 RMS 校准误差，它在理论上更有依据，但在文献中使用较少。

items.en: AUROC - We sometimes share the area under the receiver operating characteristic (AUROC) discriminating between questions models do or do not know the answer to, or discriminating between samples a model does or does not identify as correct. This captures discriminative power but is indifferent to calibration (note chance AUROC is 0.5, and larger scores are better).
items.zh: AUROC——我们有时分享区分模型知道或不知道答案的问题，或区分模型识别为正确或不正确的样本的接收者操作特征曲线下面积 (AUROC)。这捕捉了判别能力，但对校准不敏感（注意随机 AUROC 为 0.5，分数越大越好）。

items.en: (Conditional) Accuracy - When models self-evaluate the probability P(True) that their own samples are valid, we are very interested in the accuracy of the samples that models label as ‘True’ (i.e. correct), and how this compares to the accuracy on the full distribution of problems in the task.
items.zh: （条件）准确率——当模型自我评估其自身样本有效的概率 P(True) 时，我们非常关注模型标记为“True”（即正确）的样本的准确率，以及这与任务中全部问题的准确率相比如何。

items.en: Brier Score - In some cases we observe tradeoffs between discrimination (e.g. best AUROC) and calibration. Brier scores combine the discriminative power of self-evaluation with calibration (note the chance Brier score on binary choice tasks is 0.25, and smaller scores are better).
items.zh: Brier 分数——在某些情况下，我们观察到判别能力（例如最佳 AUROC）与校准之间的权衡。Brier 分数结合了自我评估的判别能力与校准（注意二选一任务中随机 Brier 分数为 0.25，分数越小越好）。

full.sec: 1.2 Models and Evaluation Tasks

items.en: Our goal in this study is to evaluate calibration and generalization on a diverse range of tasks. As such we include all of the multiple choice evaluations in BIG Bench [Srivastava et al., 2022], the MMLU evaluation [Hendrycks et al., 2021], TruthfulQA [Lin et al., 2021], LogiQA [Liu et al., 2020], and QuALITY [Pang et al., 2021]. We are most interested in open-ended generation, so we study the sampling-based evaluations TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017], Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016], the Codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021], GSM8k [Cobbe et al., 2021], some basic arithmetic problems, and a dataset of additional web-scraped Python function synthesis problems. See Appendix D for more information on our arithmetic and function synthesis datasets. Ultimately, we would like to train models to express calibrated confidence levels when generating long-form responses in natural language dialogue.
items.zh: 本研究的目标是评估多样化任务上的校准和泛化。因此，我们包含了 BIG Bench [Srivastava et al., 2022] 中的所有多项选择评估、MMLU 评估 [Hendrycks et al., 2021]、TruthfulQA [Lin et al., 2021]、LogiQA [Liu et al., 2020] 和 QuALITY [Pang et al., 2021]。我们最感兴趣的是开放式生成，因此我们研究了基于采样的评估：TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017]、Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016]、Codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021]、GSM8k [Cobbe et al., 2021]、一些基本算术问题，以及一个额外的网络抓取 Python 函数合成问题数据集。有关我们的算术和函数合成数据集的更多信息，请参见附录 D。最终，我们希望训练模型在自然语言对话中生成长篇回复时能够表达校准的置信水平。

items.en: When we perform few-shot evaluation, we simply stuff the context with randomly chosen examples from the (test) evaluation itself. For BIG Bench this means all few-shot examples come from within the specific subtask we are evaluating, though in the case of MMLU we randomize across the entire evaluation, without respecting subject boundaries.
items.zh: 当我们进行少样本评估时，我们只是将上下文填充为来自（测试）评估本身的随机选择示例。对于 BIG Bench，这意味着所有少样本示例都来自我们正在评估的特定子任务，而对于 MMLU，我们在整个评估中随机化，而不考虑学科边界。

items.en: We study a series of language models with 800M, 3B, 12B, 52B parameters. We do not include smaller models because they perform poorly on many of the evaluations we consider. The architecture and training setup for these models is identical to that in [Bai et al., 2022], except that the models we consider here were pretrained for 850B tokens, rather than the 400B tokens used in that work. For simplicity, we do not study models that have been finetuned on python code, though our pretraining distribution includes about 10% code. In section 3.3 we briefly study helpful and harmless RLHF policies finetuned (via the process in [Bai et al., 2022]) from these language models, but otherwise we only study pure language models. We show accuracies for our models on a few of the multiple choice tasks we study in Figure 37 in the appendix.
items.zh: 我们研究了一系列参数为 800M、3B、12B、52B 的语言模型。我们不包含更小的模型，因为它们在许多我们考虑的评估中表现不佳。这些模型的架构和训练设置与 [Bai et al., 2022] 中的相同，只是我们这里考虑的模型预训练了 850B 个 token，而不是该工作中使用的 400B 个 token。为简单起见，我们不研究在 Python 代码上微调的模型，尽管我们的预训练分布包含约 10% 的代码。在第 3.3 节中，我们简要研究了从这些语言模型微调（通过 [Bai et al., 2022] 中的过程）得到的有用且无害的 RLHF 策略，但除此之外，我们只研究纯语言模型。我们在附录的图 37 中展示了我们的模型在我们研究的一些多项选择任务上的准确率。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Examples of P(IK) scores from a 52B model. Token sequences that ask harder questions have lower P(IK) scores on the last token. To evaluate P(IK) on a specific full sequence, we simply take the P(IK) score at the last token. Note that we only train P(IK) on final tokens (and not on partial questions).
items.capZh: 图 3：来自 52B 模型的 P(IK) 分数示例。提出更难问题的 token 序列在最后一个 token 上的 P(IK) 分数较低。为了评估特定完整序列的 P(IK)，我们只需取最后一个 token 的 P(IK) 分数。注意，我们只在最终 token（而非部分问题）上训练 P(IK)。

full.sec: 1.3 Related Work

items.en: Calibration for general ML predictions, and interventions to improve calibration, have been studied [Nguyen and O’Connor, 2015, Hendrycks and Gimpel, 2016, Nalisnick et al., 2019, Guo et al., 2017, Hendrycks et al., 2018, Ovadia et al., 2019, Minderer et al., 2021] for some time. Calibration for language models and QA has also been studied [Desai and Durrett, 2020, Jiang et al., 2021], but typically it has been found that to achieve good calibration predictions must be adjusted. Selective prediction, where models abstain from answering certain questions, has been studied as well [Varshney et al., 2022]. Recently, the calibration of a wide range of models was analyzed on the diverse BIG Bench suite of tasks [Srivastava et al., 2022], where it was shown that language model calibration improves with model size. We are indebted to the BIG Bench collaboration for providing a convenient, huge, and diverse evaluation set. The authors of Gopher [Rae et al., 2021] briefly studied calibration on MMLU [Hendrycks et al., 2021] and found promising results, which led us to experiment with a variety of multiple choice formats.
items.zh: 通用机器学习预测的校准以及改善校准的干预措施已被研究了一段时间 [Nguyen and O’Connor, 2015, Hendrycks and Gimpel, 2016, Nalisnick et al., 2019, Guo et al., 2017, Hendrycks et al., 2018, Ovadia et al., 2019, Minderer et al., 2021]。语言模型和问答的校准也已被研究 [Desai and Durrett, 2020, Jiang et al., 2021]，但通常发现要实现良好的校准预测必须进行调整。选择性预测（模型放弃回答某些问题）也已被研究 [Varshney et al., 2022]。最近，在多样化的 BIG Bench 任务套件上分析了广泛模型的校准 [Srivastava et al., 2022]，结果表明语言模型校准随模型规模增大而改善。我们感谢 BIG Bench 合作提供了方便、庞大且多样化的评估集。Gopher 的作者 [Rae et al., 2021] 简要研究了 MMLU [Hendrycks et al., 2021] 上的校准，并发现了有希望的结果，这促使我们尝试了多种多项选择格式。

items.en: Truthfulness [Evans et al., 2021] has been a recent focus of various works, including benchmarks [Lin et al., 2021] and the incorporation of web search and citation [Nakano et al., 2021, Menick et al., 2022] into language models. That said, truthfulness focuses primarily on factual accuracy "in the world", rather than on self-knowledge, or eliciting latent knowledge [Christiano et al., 2021]. We use "honesty" [Askell et al., 2021] as an umbrella term for ideas including truthfulness, calibration, self-knowledge, explainability, and non-deceptiveness. Language models finetuned to perform non-language tasks [Ahn et al., 2022, Dinh et al., 2022] might provide an interesting test-bed for honesty in the future.
items.zh: 真实性 [Evans et al., 2021] 是近期各种工作的焦点，包括基准测试 [Lin et al., 2021] 以及将网络搜索和引用 [Nakano et al., 2021, Menick et al., 2022] 融入语言模型。尽管如此，真实性主要关注“世界中的”事实准确性，而非自我知识或引出潜在知识 [Christiano et al., 2021]。我们使用“诚实” [Askell et al., 2021] 作为涵盖真实性、校准、自我知识、可解释性和非欺骗性等概念的统称。微调以执行非语言任务的语言模型 [Ahn et al., 2022, Dinh et al., 2022] 可能为未来研究诚实性提供有趣的试验平台。

items.en: Perhaps the work most similar to ours is [Mielke et al., 2020], which is a very interesting application of metacognition/self-evaluation to improve natural language calibration. Another quite similar work is the very recent [Lin et al., 2022], where the authors train language models to express their calibration on arithmetic in words, and also study a signal analogous to P(True).
items.zh: 也许与我们的工作最相似的是 [Mielke et al., 2020]，这是一项非常有趣的元认知/自我评估在改善自然语言校准中的应用。另一项非常相似的工作是最近的 [Lin et al., 2022]，其中作者训练语言模型用语言表达其在算术上的校准，并研究了一个类似于 P(True) 的信号。

items.capEn: Figure 4: (left) We show calibration curves for various model sizes on all of the multiple choice tasks in BIG Bench, in the format described in section 2. We include a dashed line indicating perfect calibration. (right) Here we show trends in the expected calibration error on BIG Bench, for both multiple choice and a separate True/False format (see Section 3.2). We show the RMS calibration error in Figure 21 in the appendix.
items.capZh: 图 4：（左）我们展示了不同模型规模在 BIG Bench 所有多项选择任务上的校准曲线，格式如第 2 节所述。我们包含了一条虚线表示完美校准。（右）这里我们展示了 BIG Bench 上期望校准误差的趋势，包括多项选择和单独的 True/False 格式（见第 3.2 节）。我们在附录的图 21 中展示了 RMS 校准误差。

full.sec: 2 Larger Models are Calibrated on Diverse Multiple Choice Questions
full.secZh: 2 更大模型在多样化选择题上校准良好

items.en: A model makes calibrated predictions if the probability it assigns to outcomes coincides with the frequency with which these outcomes actually occur. Language models are known to produce calibrated token-level probabilities. In this section we will see that language models can produce well-calibrated probabilities when they are asked to choose the correct answer from among several explicit options. We believe calibration is interesting on its own, but it is especially relevant to honesty, since a model that can produce calibrated answers to meta-questions like ‘do you know the answer to X?’ must know something about what it knows. Generally we can use calibration as a way to bootstrap towards self-knowledge.
items.zh: 如果模型分配给结果的概率与这些结果实际发生的频率一致，则模型做出校准预测。已知语言模型能产生校准的 token 级概率。在本节中，我们将看到，当语言模型被要求从几个明确选项中选择正确答案时，它们能产生良好校准的概率。我们认为校准本身很有趣，但它与诚实性尤其相关，因为一个能够对元问题（如“你知道 X 的答案吗？”）给出校准答案的模型必须知道一些关于它自身所知的内容。通常，我们可以将校准用作引导自我知识的一种方式。

items.en: We find that when multiple choice problems are formatted in this way (as used by e.g. [Rae et al., 2021]):
items.zh: 我们发现，当多项选择题以这种方式格式化时（例如 [Rae et al., 2021] 使用的格式）：

items.en: and we identify the answers only by their labels, as e.g. ‘ (B)’, our largest models tend to produce a well-calibrated probability distribution among the available options. We show the calibration chart for all multiple choice BIG Bench tasks, in this format, in Figure 4. As can be seen in Figure 6, models are well-calibrated (in this format) even for somewhat adversarial datasets like TruthfulQA333Though note that in this format, where the model sees its options before making a choice, we do not observe anti-scaling with model size. [Lin et al., 2021], as well as for QuALITY [Pang et al., 2021] and LogiQA [Liu et al., 2020].
items.zh: 并且我们仅通过标签（如 '(B)'）来识别答案，我们最大的模型倾向于在可用选项中产生校准良好的概率分布。我们在图 4 中展示了所有 BIG Bench 多项选择题任务在此格式下的校准图。如图 6 所示，即使是对于像 TruthfulQA 这样有些对抗性的数据集，模型在此格式下也校准良好（尽管注意，在这种格式下，模型在选择之前看到选项，我们没有观察到随模型规模的反缩放）。[Lin et al., 2021]，以及 QuALITY [Pang et al., 2021] 和 LogiQA [Liu et al., 2020]。

items.capEn: Figure 5: (left) We show expected calibration error versus normalized accuracy for all BIG Bench tasks; the number of problems in each task is represented by the marker sizes. We do not find any noticeable correlation between accuracy and calibration within BIG Bench. To normalize accuracies we linearly map chance accuracy to 0, keeping perfect accuracy at 1. (right) We compare calibration for several variations on BIG Bench evaluations: we vary between 0-shot and 5-shot, replace an answer option with "none of the above", and compare our format with letter-labeled choices to the default BIG Bench formatting.
items.capZh: 图 5：（左）我们展示了所有 BIG Bench 任务的期望校准误差与归一化准确率的关系；每个任务的问题数量由标记大小表示。我们没有发现 BIG Bench 内准确率与校准之间存在显著相关性。为了归一化准确率，我们将随机猜测的准确率线性映射为 0，保持完美准确率为 1。（右）我们比较了 BIG Bench 评估的几种变体的校准：我们在 0-shot 和 5-shot 之间变化，将答案选项替换为“以上都不是”，并将我们的字母标签选项格式与默认的 BIG Bench 格式进行比较。

items.en: It is crucial that the model gets to see the answer choices explicitly before choosing amongst them; without this, we would not expect a calibrated response, due to ambiguities and degeneracies among possible paraphrases and specializations of any given answer option (e.g. "Washington" vs "George Washington, the first US president"). As can be seen in figure 5, task formatting is important for achieving excellent calibration, and calibration improves as we pass from 0-shot to 5-shot evaluation. We expect calibration is also easier to achieve with this format because each answer option corresponds to a single token (this isn’t the case in BIG Bench by default, see appendix A.4).
items.zh: 至关重要的是，模型在选择之前必须明确看到答案选项；否则，由于任何给定答案选项的可能释义和特化之间存在歧义和退化（例如“华盛顿”与“乔治·华盛顿，美国第一任总统”），我们不会期望得到校准的响应。如图 5 所示，任务格式对于实现出色的校准很重要，并且随着我们从 0-shot 评估过渡到 5-shot 评估，校准会改善。我们预计使用这种格式也更容易实现校准，因为每个答案选项对应一个 token（默认情况下 BIG Bench 并非如此，见附录 A.4）。

items.en: To simplify the interpretation of results, we reduce calibration curves to a single number by computing the expected calibration error (ECE), after binning the predictions in 10 equally-represented bins. On the right of Figure 4 we show scaling trends for calibration on BIG Bench. We typically find good calibration 0-shot, without any other prompt, though calibration improves as we pass from 0-shot to few-shot. We find that in almost all cases, calibration improves with model size and capability. Accuracy on the tasks also improves with model size, but as can be seen in Figure 5, we do not observe any obvious causal connection between accuracy and calibration. For details on how we obtain calibration charts and ECE see Appendix A.
items.zh: 为了简化结果的解释，我们将校准曲线简化为一个数字，通过将预测分到 10 个等分箱中计算期望校准误差（ECE）。在图 4 的右侧，我们展示了 BIG Bench 上校准的缩放趋势。我们通常发现 0-shot 下校准良好，无需任何其他提示，尽管校准随着我们从 0-shot 过渡到 few-shot 而改善。我们发现，在几乎所有情况下，校准随着模型规模和能力的提升而改善。任务的准确率也随模型规模提升，但如图 5 所示，我们没有观察到准确率与校准之间存在任何明显的因果联系。关于如何获得校准图和 ECE 的详细信息，请参见附录 A。

items.en: In what follows we will work to leverage these calibration results to ask language models to evaluate what they do and do not know.
items.zh: 接下来，我们将利用这些校准结果，让语言模型评估它们知道什么和不知道什么。

items.capEn: Figure 6: We show calibration curves for various model sizes on MMLU, TruthfulQA, QuALITY, and LogiQA in the format described in section 2. We emphasize that even on tasks that are difficult for LMs, like LogiQA, and on a somewhat adversarial evaluation like TruthfulQA, large models can achieve good calibration when the available answer options are presented to them in a favorable format. We include a dashed line indicating perfect calibration.
items.capZh: 图 6：我们展示了在 MMLU、TruthfulQA、QuALITY 和 LogiQA 上，采用第 2 节所述格式的各种模型大小的校准曲线。我们强调，即使在像 LogiQA 这样对语言模型困难的任务上，以及像 TruthfulQA 这样有些对抗性的评估上，当可用答案选项以有利格式呈现时，大型模型也能实现良好的校准。我们包含了一条虚线表示完美校准。

full.sec: 3 From Calibration to Knowing What You Know

items.en: If language models can answer multiple choice questions in a calibrated way, then one might hope that they can apply this ability to evaluate their own outputs. That is, we can simply ask models to generate potential answers to questions, and then have the model evaluate whether any of them are correct. In this section we will begin to explore this idea by reformulating existing tasks. Then in section 4 we will study self-evaluation.
items.zh: 如果语言模型能够以校准的方式回答多项选择题，那么人们可能希望它们能够应用这种能力来评估自己的输出。也就是说，我们可以简单地让模型生成问题的潜在答案，然后让模型评估其中是否有正确的。在本节中，我们将通过重新表述现有任务来开始探索这个想法。然后在第 4 节中，我们将研究自我评估。

full.sec: 3.1 Replacing an Option with ‘None of the Above’ Harms Performance and Calibration
full.secZh: 3.1 用“以上皆非”替换选项损害性能与校准

items.en: We have seen that language models can produce calibrated probabilities for multiple choice questions, at least when the questions and choices are provided in the right format. However, to achieve this feat the model only needs to determine the relative weight for several concrete options, when they are compared to each other.
items.zh: 我们已经看到，语言模型可以为多项选择题产生校准的概率，至少当问题和选项以正确格式提供时。然而，要实现这一壮举，模型只需要在几个具体选项相互比较时确定它们的相对权重。

items.capEn: Figure 7: (left) We show accuracy on MMLU in the standard format, and after replacing option (D) with "none of the above". This replacement decreases accuracy very significantly. (right) We show calibration specifically for the (D) answer option, in the standard form of MMLU and with (D) as "none of the above". The latter makes calibration much worse, and in particular the model seems strongly biased against using this option, which also harms accuracy.
items.capZh: 图 7：（左）我们展示了 MMLU 在标准格式下的准确率，以及将选项 (D) 替换为“以上都不是”后的准确率。这种替换显著降低了准确率。（右）我们专门展示了 MMLU 标准形式下选项 (D) 的校准，以及将 (D) 作为“以上都不是”时的校准。后者使校准变得更差，特别是模型似乎强烈偏向于不使用这个选项，这也损害了准确率。

items.en: We are more interested in whether the model actually knows whether each of the answer options is correct, when judged independently. To probe this question, we modified our multiple choice evaluations by replacing their final option with “none of the above”. This procedure can make questions that do not actually have a unique correct answer ambiguous or impossible, but for many tasks it should result in a well-defined new evaluation. In particular this procedure appears to be sensible for the vast majority of questions in MMLU. Concretely this means that we took questions such as the example in section 2 and replaced them with:
items.zh: 我们更感兴趣的是，当独立判断时，模型是否真正知道每个答案选项是否正确。为了探究这个问题，我们修改了多项选择评估，将其最后一个选项替换为“以上都不是”。这个过程可能使那些实际上没有唯一正确答案的问题变得模糊或不可能，但对于许多任务，它应该产生一个定义明确的新评估。特别是，这个过程对于 MMLU 中的绝大多数问题似乎是合理的。具体来说，我们采用了诸如第 2 节示例中的问题，并将其替换为：

items.en: We found that this procedure degraded performance very significantly on evaluations; results for MMLU are shown in Figure 36 in the appendix. Furthermore, adding "none of the above" also harms calibration, as can be seen in Figures 5 and 7. It seems that even the 52B model is biased against using the “none of the above” option and failed to use it with appropriate frequency. This is particularly surprising for 5-shot evaluations; we also tried evaluating 20-shot and this also did not improve performance.
items.zh: 我们发现这个过程显著降低了评估性能；MMLU 的结果显示在附录中的图 36。此外，添加“以上都不是”也损害了校准，如图 5 和图 7 所示。即使是 52B 模型也偏向于不使用“以上都不是”选项，并且未能以适当的频率使用它。这对于 5-shot 评估尤其令人惊讶；我们也尝试了 20-shot 评估，但性能也没有改善。

full.sec: 3.2 Models are Well-Calibrated on True/False Tasks
full.secZh: 3.2 模型在真/假任务上校准良好

items.capEn: Figure 8: We show calibration curves for various model sizes on all of the multiple choice tasks in BIG Bench, reformulated as True/False questions on a mix of the correct answers, and randomly chosen incorrect answer options. The 52B model is very well-calibrated except near the tails, where it is overconfident.
items.capZh: 图 8：我们展示了在 BIG Bench 的所有多项选择任务上，各种模型大小的校准曲线，这些任务被重新表述为关于正确答案和随机选择的错误答案选项的真/假问题。52B 模型校准良好，除了尾部附近，它过于自信。

items.en: We saw in section 3.1 that language models seem to be confused by a “none of the above” option. Here we take a different approach, and simply ask models if a given answer is true or false. So we use the format:
items.zh: 我们在第 3.1 节中看到，语言模型似乎对“以上都不是”选项感到困惑。这里我们采用不同的方法，直接询问模型给定的答案是真还是假。所以我们使用格式：

items.en: where we expect either ‘ (A)’ or ‘ (B)’ as an answer. If the model responses are correct at more than chance level, and especially if they are calibrated, then the probability P(True) indicates whether the model believes a response is valid.
items.zh: 其中我们期望答案要么是 '(A)' 要么是 '(B)'。如果模型响应正确率高于随机水平，特别是如果它们是校准的，那么概率 P(True) 表示模型是否认为响应有效。

items.en: As a first test of this approach, we can use answer options from existing multiple choice tasks. For this purpose, we take the correct answer and a randomly chosen incorrect answer, and create a new evaluation set with twice as many problems in the format above, asking models to determine if each answer is correct. In Figure 8 we show the calibration results from this method on BIG Bench. We see that the 52B model is quite well-calibrated in this context. We show similar results for MMLU in Figure 36 in the appendix, and scaling trends comparing a variety of evaluation methods in Figure 7.
items.zh: 作为这种方法的一个初步测试，我们可以使用现有多项选择任务中的答案选项。为此，我们取正确答案和一个随机选择的错误答案，并创建一个新的评估集，其中包含两倍的问题，采用上述格式，要求模型判断每个答案是否正确。在图 8 中，我们展示了这种方法在 BIG Bench 上的校准结果。我们看到 52B 模型在这种情况下校准良好。我们在附录中的图 36 中展示了 MMLU 的类似结果，并在图 7 中展示了比较各种评估方法的缩放趋势。

full.sec: 3.3 RLHF Policy Miscalibration Can Be Remediated with a Temperature Tuning
full.secZh: 3.3 RLHF策略误校准可通过温度调节修复

items.en: Our focus in this paper is on pure language models, but as a quick experiment we also looked at calibration for a helpful and harmless RLHF policy, trained exactly as in [Bai et al., 2022] using the base language models we are studying here. We find that these policies naively appear very miscalibrated, which is not surprising, since RL finetuning tends to collapse language model predictions towards behaviors that receive the most reward. However, a simple temperature adjustment (with the same temperature T=2.5𝑇2.5T=2.5 for all evaluations) largely fixes calibration issues with several independent evaluation tasks, with results shown in Figure 9. This also matches what was found for gender bias evaluations in [Bai et al., 2022], where RLHF policies had a bias score very similar to a basic language model evaluated at lower temperature. Of course more intensive RL training might distort calibration in ways that cannot be remedied in this way, but this at least provides hope that good calibration can survive some forms of finetuning.
items.zh: 本文的重点是纯语言模型，但作为快速实验，我们也研究了基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）策略的校准，该策略完全按照 [Bai et al., 2022] 使用我们正在研究的基础语言模型进行训练。我们发现这些策略在表面上看起来非常不校准，这并不奇怪，因为强化学习微调倾向于将语言模型的预测坍缩到获得最多奖励的行为上。然而，简单的温度调整（所有评估使用相同的温度 T=2.5）在很大程度上修复了几个独立评估任务的校准问题，结果如图 9 所示。这也与 [Bai et al., 2022] 中关于性别偏见评估的发现一致，其中基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）策略的偏见得分与在较低温度下评估的基本语言模型非常相似。当然，更密集的强化学习训练可能会以无法通过这种方式纠正的方式扭曲校准，但这至少提供了希望，即良好的校准可以在某些形式的微调中幸存下来。

items.capEn: Figure 9: We show calibration curves for RLHF policies finetuned from our language models. Calibration of these models appears to be very poor, but simply adjusting the temperature of their probability distributions to T=2.5𝑇2.5T=2.5 largely fixes calibration issues for three different evaluations.
items.capZh: 图 9：我们展示了从我们的语言模型微调得到的基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）策略的校准曲线。这些模型的校准似乎非常差，但简单地将它们的概率分布温度调整为 T=2.5 在很大程度上修复了三个不同评估的校准问题。

full.sec: 4 Ask the AI: Is your proposed answer True or False?
full.secZh: 4 询问AI：你提出的答案是真还是假？

items.en: We will now apply the True/False approach from section 3.2 to the samples models generated when trying to answer questions, including the short answer tasks arithmetic, Lambada, and TriviaQA, and the long-form answer tasks Codex HumanEval and GSM8k (technically GSM8k calls for a short answer, but we will be evaluating full written solution attempts, which have been solicited via chain-of-thought/scratchpad [Nye et al., 2021, Wei et al., 2022] prompting). We show the accuracies when sampling at a temperature of one in Figure 1.
items.zh: 现在，我们将第 3.2 节中的真/假方法应用于模型在尝试回答问题时的生成样本，包括短答案任务算术、Lambada 和 TriviaQA，以及长答案任务 Codex HumanEval 和 GSM8k（技术上 GSM8k 要求短答案，但我们将评估完整的书面解答尝试，这些尝试是通过思维链/草稿本提示 [Nye et al., 2021, Wei et al., 2022] 获得的）。我们在图 1 中展示了在温度为 1 时采样的准确率。

items.en: In almost all cases self-evaluation performance improves with model size, and for our 52B models answers labeled with P(True) > 50% are far more likely to be correct as compared to generic responses (see the summary histogram and comparisons in Figure 1). We also find that showing the model many T=1𝑇1T=1 samples for a single question significantly improves its ability to evaluate whether any given sample is correct. This is somewhat reminiscent of self-consistency [Wang et al., 2022], though here we are showing models their own samples, rather than externally judging self-consistency ourselves. One way to summarize these results is via the Brier scores for self-evaluation, which are shown in Figure 11.
items.zh: 在几乎所有情况下，自我评估性能随模型规模提升，对于我们的 52B 模型，标记为 P(True) > 50% 的答案比一般响应更可能正确（参见图 1 中的汇总直方图和比较）。我们还发现，对于单个问题，向模型展示许多 T=1 样本显著提高了其评估任何给定样本是否正确的能力。这有点类似于自一致性 [Wang et al., 2022]，尽管这里我们向模型展示它们自己的样本，而不是我们自己外部判断自一致性。总结这些结果的一种方式是通过自我评估的 Brier 分数，如图 11 所示。

items.en: We provide complete results in appendix C; since there are quite a few figures we only show representative examples from Lambada and Codex in the main text.
items.zh: 我们在附录C中提供了完整结果；由于图表较多，正文中仅展示来自Lambada和Codex的代表性示例。

full.sec: 4.1 Basic Self-Evaluation

items.en: In section 3.2 we saw that large language models are well-calibrated on True/False questions. Our primary motivation for studying this issue was to ask language models about their own outputs. That is, we are interested in a process where we first ask a question like:
items.zh: 在第3.2节中，我们看到大型语言模型在真/假问题上校准良好。我们研究此问题的主要动机是让语言模型询问其自身输出。也就是说，我们感兴趣的过程是：首先提出一个问题，例如：

items.en: and sample a response from the model. Then we ask the model about its own sample:
items.zh: 并从模型中采样一个回答。然后我们让模型询问其自身的采样：

items.en: and evaluate the probabilities it assigns to the ‘ (A)’ and ‘ (B)’ options. We apply this approach to model generated answers from TriviaQA [Joshi et al., 2017], Lambada [Paperno et al., 2016], the Codex HumanEval [Chen et al., 2021], GSM8k [Cobbe et al., 2021], and basic arithmetic problems.
items.zh: 并评估它分配给“(A)”和“(B)”选项的概率。我们将此方法应用于TriviaQA [Joshi等人，2017]、Lambada [Paperno等人，2016]、Codex HumanEval [Chen等人，2021]、GSM8k [Cobbe等人，2021]以及基本算术问题中模型生成的答案。

items.capEn: Figure 10: Models self-evaluate their own samples by producing a probability P(True) that the samples are in fact correct. Here we show histograms of P(True) for the correct and incorrect samples, in the evaluation paradigm where models also see five T=1𝑇1T=1 samples for the same question, in order to improve their judgment. Here we show results only for Lambada and Codex, as these are fairly representative of short-answer and long-answer behavior; for full results see Figure 28 in the appendix.
items.capZh: 图10：模型通过生成概率P(True)来自我评估其样本是否正确。这里我们展示了正确和错误样本的P(True)直方图，评估范式为模型同时看到同一问题的五个T=1样本，以改进其判断。此处仅展示Lambada和Codex的结果，因为它们相当代表短答案和长答案行为；完整结果见附录图28。

items.en: This True/False self-evaluation may be considerably more difficult than the tasks we studied in section 3.2, because there the model was presented with human-written possibilities, whereas here the model is forced to evaluate444Language models do not typically have an explicit way of knowing if tokens were provided to them by a third party, or are their own samples. This may make self-evaluation more difficult (or at least ambiguous), and can have other issues in an RL setting [Ortega et al., 2021]. its own samples. These samples may lie close to the model’s own decision boundary for validity (or the model may simply be overconfident about its own samples), whereas options presented to the model by a third party may be easier to categorize. We observe this effect in several ways. Zero-shot, P(True) is poorly calibrated, and typically it lies close to 50% for typical samples (see Figure 38 in the appendix for illustrations). Furthermore, we show in Figure 33 in the appendix that samples from smaller models are universally easier to categorize as correct or incorrect, for models of all sizes.
items.zh: 这种真/假自我评估可能比我们在第3.2节中研究的任务困难得多，因为在那里模型看到的是人类编写的可能性，而这里模型被迫评估其自身样本。这些样本可能接近模型自身的有效性决策边界（或者模型可能对其自身样本过度自信），而第三方呈现给模型的选项可能更容易分类。我们通过多种方式观察到这种效应。零样本情况下，P(True)校准不良，且对于典型样本通常接近50%（见附录图38的示例）。此外，我们在附录图33中显示，对于所有规模的模型，来自较小模型的样本普遍更容易被分类为正确或错误。

items.capEn: Figure 11: Here we show results only for Lambada and Codex, as these are fairly representative of short-answer and long-answer behavior; for full results see Figures 28, 29, 30, and 31 in the appendix. Top: Here we show the Brier scores for model self-evaluation with three methods: basic self-evaluation with a prompt, and self-evaluation with comparison samples, either with a fixed prompt or 20-shot. Note that the Brier score combines accuracy of the True/False determination with calibration, and 20-shot evaluation with comparison samples performs best in every case. Brier scores do not decrease with model size on evaluations like Codex because small model samples are almost always invalid, so that it’s relatively trivial to achieve a small Brier score. Bottom: We show the base accuracy of our models on various sampling tasks, and then the accuracy among the responses where via self-evaluation we have P(True) >0.5absent0.5>0.5. For P(True) we evaluate with a single example and a prompt, and then both 20-shot and with a prompt with five comparison examples. Few-shot evaluation is important for obtaining good calibration.
items.capZh: 图11：此处仅展示Lambada和Codex的结果，因为它们相当代表短答案和长答案行为；完整结果见附录图28、29、30和31。上方：我们展示了三种模型自我评估方法的Brier分数：带提示的基本自我评估，以及带比较样本的自我评估（固定提示或20-shot）。注意Brier分数结合了真/假判定的准确性和校准度，20-shot带比较样本的评估在所有情况下表现最佳。在Codex等评估中，Brier分数不随模型规模减小，因为小模型的样本几乎总是无效，从而相对容易获得较小的Brier分数。下方：我们展示了模型在各种采样任务上的基础准确率，以及通过自我评估P(True)>0.5的回答中的准确率。对于P(True)，我们使用单个示例和提示进行评估，然后使用20-shot和带五个比较示例的提示。少样本评估对于获得良好校准至关重要。

full.sec: 4.2 Showing Many T=1𝑇1T=1 Samples Improves Self-Evaluation
full.secZh: 4.2 展示多个T=1样本改进自我评估

items.en: We can improve performance further by showing the model other T=1𝑇1T=1 samples, for comparison. That is, we generate 5 samples in total, and then ask the model about the validity of one of these samples:
items.zh: 我们可以通过向模型展示其他T=1样本来进一步改进性能。也就是说，我们总共生成5个样本，然后询问模型其中一个样本的有效性：

items.en: With this format, performance improves significantly on all of the short-form answer tasks, as compared to the version where we only show models a single proposed answer, as summarized in Figure 11. However, models benefit less from this approach on tasks requiring long-form answers (Codex and GMS8k). As noted in the last section, calibration of P(True) is relatively poor zero-shot, but it improves few-shot. In Figure 32 in the appendix we show the full calibration curves for 20-shot True/False self-evaluation.
items.zh: 使用这种格式，与仅向模型展示单个提议答案的版本相比，所有短答案任务上的性能显著提升，如图11总结。然而，模型在需要长答案的任务（Codex和GSM8k）上从这种方法中获益较少。如上一节所述，P(True)的校准在零样本下相对较差，但在少样本下有所改善。在附录图32中，我们展示了20-shot真/假自我评估的完整校准曲线。

items.en: A pragmatic comparison of self-evaluation techniques can be seen on the bottom of Figure 11. There we directly compare the overall accuracy of our models at the task, to the accuracies for the subset of responses where the models chose P(True) >0.5absent0.5>0.5. We see that these conditional accuracies are substantially higher than the overall accuracy, with the separation between base and conditional accuracies growing with model size. This suggests that as capabilities improve and samples become more sophisticated, models seem to demonstrate relative improvement at self-evaluation, compared to their generative ability. This accords with the intuitive sense that verification is often easier than generation.
items.zh: 自我评估技术的实用比较见图11下方。在那里我们直接比较了模型在任务上的总体准确率，与模型选择P(True)>0.5的回答子集的准确率。我们看到这些条件准确率显著高于总体准确率，且基础准确率与条件准确率之间的差距随模型规模增大而增大。这表明随着能力提升和样本变得更加复杂，模型在自我评估方面似乎表现出相对改进，相比其生成能力。这与验证通常比生成更容易的直觉一致。

items.en: Overall, if given a few examples from a given distribution, models can generate samples and then self-evaluate them to productively differentiate correct and incorrect samples, with reasonably calibrated confidence.
items.zh: 总体而言，如果给定来自特定分布的少量示例，模型可以生成样本，然后自我评估它们，以有效区分正确和错误样本，并具有合理校准的置信度。

full.sec: 5 Training Models to Predict Whether They Can Answer Questions Correctly
full.secZh: 5 训练模型预测能否正确回答问题

items.en: In this section, we will train models to predict whether they know the answer to any given free-form question, denoting the probability they assign as ‘P(IK)’ (for Probability that I Know the answer). This is fundamentally a question about the model itself, as different models will know the answers to different questions.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们将训练模型预测它们是否知道任何给定自由形式问题的答案，将分配的概率记为“P(IK)”（表示我知道答案的概率）。这本质上是一个关于模型自身的问题，因为不同模型知道不同问题的答案。

items.en: Value Head: We train P(IK) as the logit from an additional value ‘head’ added to the model (independent of the logits for language modeling). An advantage of this approach is that we can easily probe P(IK) at general token positions.
items.zh: 价值头：我们将P(IK)训练为添加到模型上的额外“价值”头的logit（独立于语言建模的logits）。这种方法的一个优点是我们可以轻松地在一般token位置探测P(IK)。

items.en: Natural Language: We train P(IK) by asking the model to literally address "With what confidence could you answer this question?", and output an answer like 0%, 10%, 20%, ⋯⋯\cdots 100%.
items.zh: 自然语言：我们通过让模型字面回答“你能以多大置信度回答这个问题？”并输出如0%、10%、20%、⋯100%的答案来训练P(IK)。

items.en: We had hoped to observe large benefits from few-shot evaluation out-of-distribution with the natural language approach. In early experiments we did not observe major gains, and so we will use the value head approach in what follows. But we believe it would be worth returning to the natural language approach in the future.
items.zh: 我们曾希望从自然语言方法的少样本评估中观察到巨大的分布外收益。在早期实验中我们没有观察到显著提升，因此我们将在后续中使用价值头方法。但我们认为未来值得重新审视自然语言方法。

items.en: For each question, we generated 30 answer samples at T=1𝑇1T=1. For a given question Q𝑄Q, if 20 of the model’s sampled answers were correct, and 10 were incorrect, our training set contained 20 copies of the (Q,IK)𝑄IK(Q,\text{IK}) datapoint and 10 copies of the (Q,IDK)𝑄IDK(Q,\text{IDK}) datapoint. This was a convenience to allow us to easily approximate555In some cases P(IK) could be somewhat misleading – for instance, Owain Evans pointed out the nice example ”Name a composite integer in the range [1,100]?” where by chance the model may achieve an apparent P(IK) =0.75absent0.75=0.75 simply by randomly sampling integers in [1,100]. a soft label for the ground-truth P(IK) by using many hard labels. We trained with a cross-entropy loss on these labels.
items.zh: 对于每个问题，我们在T=1下生成了30个答案样本。对于给定问题Q，如果模型的20个采样答案正确，10个错误，则我们的训练集包含20个(Q,IK)数据点和10个(Q,IDK)数据点。这是一种便利方法，使我们能够通过使用多个硬标签轻松近似真实P(IK)的软标签。我们使用交叉熵损失对这些标签进行训练。

full.sec: 5.1 Evaluating P(IK) Training and Model Size Trends
full.secZh: 5.1 评估P(IK)训练与模型规模趋势

items.en: Because we’re adding a new untrained head, language models do not perform well zero or few-shot at predicting P(IK), so we need to finetune them. Since TriviaQA has a large training set, we explore generalization by finetuning P(IK) only on trivia questions. In order to obtain ground-truth P(IK) scores that we use for training, we sample 30 answers (at temperature =1absent1=1) from each TriviaQA question with a 10-shot prompt constructed from other random TriviaQA questions and answers. We use a 10-shot prompt simply to ensure that the models almost always output answers in the correct format, which is important because correctness on TriviaQA questions is judged based on a string comparison.
items.zh: 由于我们添加了一个新的未训练头，语言模型在零样本或少样本下预测P(IK)表现不佳，因此我们需要微调它们。由于TriviaQA有大量训练集，我们仅通过微调P(IK)在常识问题上来探索泛化。为了获得用于训练的真实P(IK)分数，我们从每个TriviaQA问题中采样30个答案（温度为1），使用由其他随机TriviaQA问题和答案构建的10-shot提示。我们使用10-shot提示只是为了确保模型几乎总是以正确格式输出答案，这对于基于字符串比较判断TriviaQA问题的正确性很重要。

items.en: Our dataset for training and evaluating the classifier then contains datapoints of the form (Few-Shot Prompt + Question, Ground Truth Label). During P(IK) training, we finetune the entire model along with the value head. In Figure 12, we then evaluate this classifier on a held-out set of TriviaQA test questions, and see that the model is able to separate the questions it got correct and incorrect quite well. In particular, P(IK) is very well calibrated on TriviaQA. As a note, in a later section we train P(IK) on Lambada, Arithmetic, and Python Function Synthesis problems as well. We used a 10-shot prompt for both Lambada and Arithmetic, while Python Function Synthesis was done 0-shot.
items.zh: 我们用于训练和评估分类器的数据集包含形式为（少样本提示+问题，真实标签）的数据点。在P(IK)训练期间，我们微调整个模型以及价值头。在图12中，我们在保留的TriviaQA测试问题集上评估此分类器，看到模型能够很好地区分它回答正确和错误的问题。特别是，P(IK)在TriviaQA上校准非常好。作为说明，在后续章节中我们也在Lambada、算术和Python函数合成问题上训练P(IK)。我们对Lambada和算术使用10-shot提示，而Python函数合成为0-shot。

items.capEn: Figure 12: Testing a 52B classifier on a held-out set of TriviaQA questions. We see that the classifier predicts lower values of P(IK) for the questions it gets incorrect, and higher values of P(IK) for the questions it gets correct. We set the ground truth P(IK) as the fraction of samples at T=1𝑇1T=1 that the model gets correct.
items.capZh: 图12：在保留的TriviaQA问题集上测试52B分类器。我们看到分类器对模型回答错误的问题预测较低的P(IK)值，对回答正确的问题预测较高的P(IK)值。我们将真实P(IK)设置为模型在T=1下正确样本的比例。

items.capEn: Figure 13: Left: Full calibration plot of P(IK) classifiers on TriviaQA over a range of model sizes. We see that the smallest models have higher calibration error than the biggest models. The larger classifiers are very well calibrated in-distribution. Right: We show calibration curves for P(IK) on three other sampling-based datasets, both in-distribution and out-of-distribution (trained only on TriviaQA). We see that OOD calibration of P(IK) is often quite poor, and for the most part models are underconfident.
items.capZh: 图13：左图：TriviaQA上不同规模模型的P(IK)分类器完整校准图。我们看到最小模型的校准误差大于最大模型。较大分类器在分布内校准非常好。右图：我们展示了其他三个基于采样的数据集上P(IK)的校准曲线，包括分布内和分布外（仅在TriviaQA上训练）。我们看到P(IK)的分布外校准通常很差，且大多数情况下模型过于不自信。

full.sec: 5.2 Out of Distribution Generalization of P(IK)

items.en: Next, we study the generalization of P(IK) when training only on TriviaQA and then evaluating on Lambada, Arithmetic, GSM8k, Codex HumanEval, and a set of ‘naturally occurring’ python function synthesis tasks scraped from GitHub (we added this test set because the Codex evaluation is not very large). We are interested in how generalization scales across model sizes, and how performance compares to the case where we do train P(IK) on these distributions.
items.zh: 接下来，我们研究仅使用TriviaQA训练P(IK)后，在Lambada、Arithmetic、GSM8k、Codex HumanEval以及一组从GitHub抓取的“自然出现”的Python函数合成任务（我们添加了这个测试集，因为Codex评估规模不够大）上的泛化情况。我们关注泛化如何随模型规模扩展，以及性能与在这些分布上训练P(IK)的情况相比如何。

items.en: We treat GSM8k slightly differently, since it was harder than other tasks. As for TriviaQA, we generated 30 samples @ T=1𝑇1T=1 per question from GSM8k using a 10-shot prompt of examples to ensure proper formatting. However, the 10-shot prompt for GSM8k included reasoning, as well as the final answer. This meant that each of the 30 samples included model-generated reasoning. Similarly to other tasks, we calculate a ground-truth P(IK) for each question as just the fraction of T=1𝑇1T=1 samples that are correct. However, since there aren’t many problems with a ground truth P​(I​K)>0.5𝑃𝐼𝐾0.5P(IK)>0.5, we sometimes binarize (i.e. split the distribution) using a threshold of 0.25, instead of 0.5. We only evaluate the largest model (52B) on GSM8k due to its difficulty.
items.zh: 我们对GSM8k的处理略有不同，因为它比其他任务更难。对于TriviaQA，我们使用10-shot提示示例从GSM8k的每个问题生成30个样本（T=1），以确保格式正确。然而，GSM8k的10-shot提示包含推理过程和最终答案。这意味着每个30个样本都包含模型生成的推理。与其他任务类似，我们计算每个问题的真实P(IK)，即T=1样本中正确的比例。但由于真实P(IK)>0.5的问题不多，我们有时使用0.25的阈值进行二值化（即分割分布），而不是0.5。由于GSM8k的难度，我们只评估最大模型（52B）。

items.capEn: Figure 14: Model size trend of AUROC with P(IK), when training on only TriviaQA. We generally observe increasing AUROC as model size increases for all three out-of-distribution evals, suggesting that larger P(IK) classifiers are better at generalization.
items.capZh: 图14：仅使用TriviaQA训练时，AUROC随模型大小与P(IK)的关系。我们普遍观察到，随着模型大小增加，所有三个分布外评估的AUROC均上升，表明更大的P(IK)分类器泛化能力更强。

items.capEn: Figure 15: Calibration on GSM8k when training on only TriviaQA vs training on TriviaQA, LAMBADA, Mixed-Arithmetic, and Python Function Synthesis. We see that calibration does improve when training on more diverse data, rather instead of being mostly underconfident the model becomes overconfident. Note that GSM8k was significantly out of the training distribution in all cases.
items.capZh: 图15：仅使用TriviaQA训练与使用TriviaQA、LAMBADA、混合算术和Python函数合成训练时，GSM8k上的校准效果。我们看到，在更多样化的数据上训练时校准确实有所改善，模型从主要欠自信变为过度自信。注意，在所有情况下GSM8k都显著偏离训练分布。

items.capEn: Figure 16: Generalization of P(IK). We trained P(IK) classifiers on just TriviaQA and on TriviaQA, Arithmetic, Python Function Synthesis, and LAMBADA. The left side of this figure includes distributions of P(IK) from a 52B classifier that was trained on just TriviaQA, while the right side includes distributions of P(IK) from a 52B classifier that was trained on all of these data distributions. We observe nontrivial generalization from TriviaQA to the other tasks, but training on the other tasks improves performance greatly.
items.capZh: 图16：P(IK)的泛化。我们仅在TriviaQA上以及同时在TriviaQA、算术、Python函数合成和LAMBADA上训练了P(IK)分类器。该图左侧包含仅使用TriviaQA训练的52B分类器的P(IK)分布，右侧包含使用所有这些数据分布训练的52B分类器的P(IK)分布。我们观察到从TriviaQA到其他任务存在非平凡的泛化，但在其他任务上训练显著提升了性能。

items.capEn: Figure 17: Left: P(IK) scores on GSM8k when training on only TriviaQA. Right: P(IK) scores on GSM8k when training on TriviaQA, LAMBADA, Mixed-Arithmetic, and Python Function Synthesis. Note the threshold for Ground Truth P(IK) is 0.25 here, in contrast to 0.5 in the other histograms from Figure 16 and Table 1
items.capZh: 图17：左：仅使用TriviaQA训练时GSM8k上的P(IK)分数。右：使用TriviaQA、LAMBADA、混合算术和Python函数合成训练时GSM8k上的P(IK)分数。注意，此处真实P(IK)的阈值为0.25，与图16和表1中其他直方图的0.5不同。

full.sec: 5.3 P(IK) Generalizes to Account for Source Materials

items.en: If we consider a fairly obscure question like
items.zh: 如果我们考虑一个相当晦涩的问题，

items.en: then P(IK) appropriately predicts a low value, specifically 18% for a 52B model. However, if we prepend a Wikipedia article on the Idaho Rodeo Hall of Fame to the context:
items.zh: 那么P(IK)会适当预测一个低值，对于52B模型具体为18%。然而，如果我们在上下文中添加一篇关于爱达荷牛仔竞技名人堂的维基百科文章：

items.en: then the P(IK) score rises to 78%. In other words, without any further training, P(IK) generalizes to address whether the language model can find answers to questions in source materials within its context.
items.zh: 那么P(IK)分数上升到78%。换句话说，无需进一步训练，P(IK)就能泛化以判断语言模型是否能在其上下文中的源材料中找到问题的答案。

items.en: We demonstrate this phenomenon quantitatively using questions from TriviaQA, by comparing P(IK) evaluated both with and without accompanying reference material. Questions from TriviaQA come with Wikipedia documents that are relevant to each questions. For each question, we then compute P(IK) both with an without the reference document. Specifically, the format for the prompts are as follows:
items.zh: 我们使用TriviaQA的问题定量展示了这一现象，通过比较有无附带参考材料时评估的P(IK)。TriviaQA的问题附带了与每个问题相关的维基百科文档。对于每个问题，我们计算有无参考文档时的P(IK)。具体来说，提示的格式如下：

items.en: Whenever the full article was excessively long, we needed to truncate it to 7000 tokens so that the article and the question would always fit in the model context. Figure 18 summarizes the effects of including Wikipedia articles on P(IK). We see that including the article increases P(IK). Furthermore, shorter articles increase P(IK) more. Presumably this is because the correct answer is easier to extract from shorter articles than longer articles.
items.zh: 每当完整文章过长时，我们需要将其截断至7000个token，以便文章和问题总能放入模型上下文中。图18总结了包含维基百科文章对P(IK)的影响。我们看到包含文章会增加P(IK)。此外，较短的文章增加P(IK)更多。这可能是因为从较短的文章中提取正确答案比较长的文章更容易。

items.capEn: Figure 18: Effect of including Wikipedia article on P(IK) for TriviaQA Questions. We see that including a relevant Wikipedia article in the context boosts the average P(IK) on TriviaQA Questions. P(IK) increases more for shorter Wikipedia articles, from which it is presumably easier to identify the relevant facts.
items.capZh: 图18：包含维基百科文章对TriviaQA问题P(IK)的影响。我们看到在上下文中包含相关的维基百科文章会提高TriviaQA问题的平均P(IK)。较短的维基百科文章使P(IK)增加更多，从中可能更容易识别相关事实。

full.sec: 5.4 P(IK) Generalizes to Account for Hints Towards GSM8k Solutions

items.en: In this section we study how hints towards the solution of GSM8k problems affect P(IK) scores. Specifically, we add hints to the problem statement using the following format:
items.zh: 在本节中，我们研究GSM8k问题的提示如何影响P(IK)分数。具体来说，我们使用以下格式向问题陈述中添加提示：

items.en: We test two variations on hints. First, we vary the amount of information given away by the hint by truncating hints at fixed fractions of their total length. Second, we compare how P(IK) is affected by good, incorrect, and distracting hints. Bad hints are acquired by generating chain-of-thought samples from our 52B model and selecting only the samples that lead to an incorrect answer. Distracting hints are simply hints that were generated for a different question, so that they are irrelevant to the question at hand.
items.zh: 我们测试了两种提示变体。首先，我们通过将提示截断到其总长度的固定比例来改变提示所透露的信息量。其次，我们比较了好的、错误的和干扰性提示对P(IK)的影响。错误提示是通过从我们的52B模型生成思维链样本并仅选择导致错误答案的样本获得的。干扰性提示只是为另一个问题生成的提示，因此与当前问题无关。

items.capEn: Figure 19: Effect of hints on P(IK) applied to GSM8k – all of these results represent generalization, as the models were not trained on GSM8k. Left: We see that showing more of the GSM8k hint results in higher P(IK). The effect is more consistent for the model trained on all 4 tasks (TriviaQA, LAMBADA, Arithmetic, and Python Function Synthesis), rather than the one trained on just TriviaQA. Right: We evaluate the model trained on all 4 other tasks on various hints. We see lower P(IK) scores for bad hints (though the models are partially fooled), and actual decreases in the P(IK) score when the hints are irrelevant because they come from other questions.
items.capZh: 图19：提示对GSM8k上P(IK)的影响——所有这些结果都代表泛化，因为模型未在GSM8k上训练。左：我们看到显示更多GSM8k提示会导致更高的P(IK)。对于在所有4个任务（TriviaQA、LAMBADA、算术和Python函数合成）上训练的模型，这种效果比仅在TriviaQA上训练的模型更一致。右：我们评估了在所有其他4个任务上训练的模型对各种提示的反应。我们看到错误提示的P(IK)分数较低（尽管模型部分被欺骗），而当提示来自其他问题而无关时，P(IK)分数实际下降。

full.sec: 5.5 Comparing Models Trained with Distinct Pretraining Distributions
full.secZh: 5.5 比较不同预训练分布训练的模型

items.en: We would like P(IK) to truly capture model self-knowledge. Thus we would like to distinguish between this and an alternative hypothesis, that P(IK) is merely capturing something like the intrinsic difficulty of tasks. In order to try to disentangle these explanations, we studied two 12B models (with identical architecture) that were pretrained on distinct data distributions – one was trained with four repetitions of a high quality dataset, while the other uses a single copy of that dataset mixed with a single copy of a larger but lower-quality distribution of webdata. We refer to these pretrained language models as A𝐴A and B𝐵B in this section. We finetuned both models for P(IK) on the questions from the TriviaQA training set that each model got correct.
items.zh: 我们希望P(IK)真正捕捉模型的自我知识。因此，我们希望将其与另一种假设区分开来，即P(IK)仅仅捕捉任务的固有难度。为了尝试解开这些解释，我们研究了两个12B模型（具有相同架构），它们在不同数据分布上预训练——一个使用高质量数据集的四个重复进行训练，而另一个使用该数据集的一个副本与一个更大但质量较低的网页数据分布的一个副本混合。在本节中，我们将这些预训练语言模型称为A和B。我们针对TriviaQA训练集中每个模型正确回答的问题对两个模型进行了P(IK)微调。

items.en: These models get many of the same questions correct and incorrect. So our focus here will be on the subset of questions that one model answers correctly and the other incorrectly. In the TriviaQA test set there were 650 and 826 such questions, respectively, that model A or B got correct but that the other got wrong. Table 2 shows that each model has a noticeably higher P(IK) for the questions that they get correct, compared to the questions that the other model gets right. In Figure 20 we show a scatter plot of P(IK) for models A and B, colored by which model got the questions right. The differences between the distributions are small but noticeable.
items.zh: 这些模型在许多相同的问题上正确和错误。因此，我们的重点将放在一个模型正确回答而另一个模型错误回答的问题子集上。在TriviaQA测试集中，分别有650和826个这样的问题，模型A或B正确回答而另一个错误。表2显示，每个模型对自己正确回答的问题的P(IK)明显高于对另一个模型正确回答的问题。在图20中，我们展示了模型A和B的P(IK)散点图，按哪个模型正确回答了问题着色。分布之间的差异虽小但明显。

items.en: We also tried a second experiment, in order to try to get insight as to whether P(IK) leverages features specifically relevant to self-knowledge. For this purpose we finetuned both the A𝐴A and B𝐵B pretrained models on the ground-truth P(IK) data from each model. So for example, we started from model A𝐴A, and finetuned it on the ground-truth P(IK) data from model A𝐴A and, separately, on the data from model B𝐵B (resulting in two distinct finetuned snapshots). We might hope to find that training a P(IK) classifier to predict whether or not model A𝐴A knows the answer to a question would work better when starting from model A𝐴A itself as the initial checkpoint, as compared to if we start from model B𝐵B. Table 3 contains results for this experiment.
items.zh: 我们还尝试了第二个实验，以了解P(IK)是否利用了与自我知识特别相关的特征。为此，我们根据每个模型的真实P(IK)数据对A和B预训练模型进行了微调。例如，我们从模型A开始，分别根据模型A的真实P(IK)数据和模型B的真实P(IK)数据对其进行微调（得到两个不同的微调快照）。我们可能希望发现，从模型A本身作为初始检查点开始训练P(IK)分类器来预测模型A是否知道问题的答案，会比从模型B开始效果更好。表3包含了该实验的结果。

items.en: We find mixed but encouraging results here: when testing on the ground-truth P(IK) data from model A, both starting from model A and starting from model B seems to give comparable performance. However, when testing on ground-truth P(IK) data from model B, starting from model B seems to do better than starting from model A. We also tried focusing on only the questions where the two models differ, but we found similar results in that case.
items.zh: 我们在这里发现了混合但令人鼓舞的结果：当在模型A的真实P(IK)数据上测试时，从模型A开始和从模型B开始似乎都给出了相当的性能。然而，当在模型B的真实P(IK)数据上测试时，从模型B开始似乎比从模型A开始更好。我们还尝试只关注两个模型有分歧的问题，但在那种情况下发现了类似的结果。

items.capEn: Figure 20: Scatterplot that disambiguates numbers in Table 2. We evaluate 2 distinct 12B models on TriviaQA, separating the data into questions each model gets correct and the other gets incorrect. The scatterplot depicts the P(IK) scores from each model on both of these data subsets.
items.capZh: 图20：区分表2中数字的散点图。我们在TriviaQA上评估两个不同的12B模型，将数据分为每个模型正确而另一个错误的问题。散点图描绘了每个模型在这两个数据子集上的P(IK)分数。

full.sec: 6 Discussion

items.en: The long-term motivation underlying this work is to begin to understand aspects of honesty in AI models. We use honesty as an umbrella term including several overlapping777We are not attempting a precise taxonomy, but simply pointing at a collection of important ideas. ideas:
items.zh: 这项工作的长期动机是开始理解AI模型在诚实性方面的某些方面。我们将诚实性作为一个总括术语，涵盖几个重叠的想法（我们并非试图进行精确的分类，而只是指向一系列重要概念）：

items.en: Truthfulness: Does the AI provide factually accurate information, including finding, using, and evaluating source materials correctly?
items.zh: 真实性：AI是否提供事实准确的信息，包括正确查找、使用和评估源材料？

items.en: Calibration: Do the AI’s probabilistic predictions correspond with frequencies of occurrence?
items.zh: 校准：AI的概率预测是否与事件发生的频率相对应？

items.en: Self-knowledge: Do AI systems know what they know and make accurate predictions about their own behavior and reasoning?
items.zh: 自我认知：AI系统是否知道它们知道什么，并对其自身行为和推理做出准确预测？

items.en: Explainability: Do AI systems reveal their ‘thinking’ completely and faithfully?
items.zh: 可解释性：AI系统是否完全且忠实地揭示其“思考”过程？

items.en: Non-deceptiveness: Can we ensure AI systems do not learn to lie (e.g. when human preference data might encourage systematic mistakes or provide more reward for pleasant misconceptions)?
items.zh: 非欺骗性：我们能否确保AI系统不学会撒谎（例如，当人类偏好数据可能鼓励系统性错误或为令人愉快的误解提供更多奖励时）？

items.en: Here we have focused on aspects of calibration, self-knowledge, and truthfulness, but we are only scratching the surface of the larger subject. Our core findings are that large language models can be well-calibrated on diverse multiple choice questions, that they perform well at self-evaluation on a range of subjects, and that they can be trained to predict what they do and don’t know and exhibit some generalization to new domains and in-context information sources.
items.zh: 本文重点关注校准、自我认知和真实性方面，但我们只是触及了这个更大主题的表面。我们的核心发现是，大型语言模型在多样化的多项选择题上可以很好地校准，在多个主题的自我评估中表现良好，并且可以训练它们预测自己知道和不知道的内容，并在新领域和上下文信息源中表现出一定的泛化能力。

items.en: We found that calibration tends to improve with model size/capability and with additional few-shot examples. Self-evaluation also improves with model size, which is non-trivial since we expect the quality of model samples to also be improving as we scale up. This means that in effect, we are observing that verification has an advantage over generation. Within the scope of pretrained language models, we find these results encouraging, and expect they can be usefully leveraged to bootstrap more extensive forms of self or inter-model supervision, and to distill calibration back into open-ended natural language responses.
items.zh: 我们发现，校准往往随着模型规模/能力的增加以及更多少样本示例而改善。自我评估也随着模型规模的增大而改善，这并非微不足道，因为我们预计模型样本的质量也会随着规模扩大而提高。这意味着，实际上我们观察到验证相对于生成具有优势。在预训练语言模型的范围内，我们认为这些结果令人鼓舞，并期望它们可以有效地用于引导更广泛的自我或模型间监督形式，并将校准提炼回开放式的自然语言响应中。

items.en: Another motivation for this work was to use self-knowledge as a test-bed for generalization. We are particularly interested in how honesty generalizes from easy to hard questions and across domains, because we are worried about how AI systems may behave out-of-distribution, or when they have some knowledge or insight that humans lack. We found that language models do exhibit a degree of generalization across domains, though calibration suffers out-of-distribution. We also found that although models are trained to predict P(IK) for standalone questions, they generalize in such a way that P(IK) increases significantly when models are provided with source materials that contain the answers to these questions, or hints towards the solution of math word problems. This suggests a relationship between the way models store longterm memory and the way they use information within their contexts, i.e. it suggests a connection between learning and in-context learning.
items.zh: 这项工作的另一个动机是利用自我认知作为泛化的测试平台。我们特别感兴趣的是诚实性如何从简单问题泛化到困难问题以及跨领域，因为我们担心AI系统在分布外或当它们拥有人类缺乏的知识或洞察力时可能如何行为。我们发现，语言模型确实表现出一定程度的跨领域泛化，尽管校准在分布外会受到影响。我们还发现，尽管模型被训练为预测独立问题的P(IK)，但当模型被提供包含这些问题答案的源材料或数学文字题解决方案的提示时，P(IK)会显著增加，这表明模型存储长期记忆的方式与它们在上下文中使用信息的方式之间存在关系，即它暗示了学习与上下文学习之间的联系。

full.sec: 6.1 Limitations and Future Work

items.en: We have focused on pretrained language models, but models that have been finetuned for a specific purpose, such as RLHF policies [Bai et al., 2022], will not be so well-calibrated, and some of the methods we discussed may not work as well.
items.zh: 我们专注于预训练语言模型，但针对特定目的微调的模型，例如基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）策略[Bai et al., 2022]，将不会如此良好地校准，我们讨论的一些方法可能效果不佳。

items.en: Furthermore, language model pretraining is a form of human imitation. Some of the most interesting questions about honesty concern setups where advanced AI systems ‘know’ something that clashes with or extends what humans know, and so cannot have been grasped from pretraining. Our analysis does not differentiate between "the truth" and "what humans say", but eventually we would like to train AI systems that recognize this distinction and generalize appropriately.
items.zh: 此外，语言模型预训练是一种人类模仿形式。关于诚实性的一些最有趣的问题涉及高级AI系统“知道”某些与人类所知冲突或超出人类所知的事情，因此无法从预训练中掌握。我们的分析没有区分“真相”和“人类所说的话”，但最终我们希望训练AI系统识别这种区别并适当泛化。

items.en: This work does not address the possibility that AI systems may learn to behave deceptively (in order to e.g. maximize human preference rewards), and intentional deception would counteract simple trends in honest self-evaluation.
items.zh: 这项工作没有解决AI系统可能学会欺骗性行为（例如，为了最大化人类偏好奖励）的可能性，而故意欺骗会抵消诚实自我评估的简单趋势。

items.en: We were interested in the generalization of honesty because we would like to predict whether AI systems will extrapolate from training to exhibit safe and desirable behavior in new contexts. We found some interesting and potentially encouraging forms of generalization. But we only studied five sampling-based datasets, and our observations concerning capability trends and generalization were limited in power and scope. It would be interesting to investigate these more thoroughly and precisely.
items.zh: 我们对诚实性的泛化感兴趣，因为我们希望预测AI系统是否会从训练中推断出在新环境中表现出安全且理想的行为。我们发现了一些有趣且可能令人鼓舞的泛化形式。但我们只研究了五个基于采样的数据集，并且我们关于能力趋势和泛化的观察在能力和范围上有限。更彻底和精确地研究这些将是有趣的。

items.en: With that said, we believe that the simple methods we have demonstrated here lay the foundation for investigating some of these issues in the future.
items.zh: 尽管如此，我们相信我们在此展示的简单方法为未来研究其中一些问题奠定了基础。

full.sec: 6.2 Broader Impacts

items.en: We hope that the broader impacts of this work will be positive. Pretrained language models and other AI systems can be dishonest in a number of ways, and we hope that this work will make it easier to identify and correct failures of honesty in deployed systems. That said, pretrained language models may have other failure modes, and the interventions we study here may not correct errors that are perpetuated by language model training data and human imitation.
items.zh: 我们希望这项工作的更广泛影响将是积极的。预训练语言模型和其他AI系统可能以多种方式不诚实，我们希望这项工作将使识别和纠正部署系统中诚实性失败变得更容易。也就是说，预训练语言模型可能有其他失败模式，我们在此研究的干预措施可能无法纠正由语言模型训练数据和人类模仿所延续的错误。

full.sec: A.1 Calibration Charts

items.en: To evaluate calibration on multiple choice problems, we obtain a list of all probabilities for all answer options (both correct and incorrect choices). We then sort these probabilities and bin them into 10 groups, each with an equal number of probabilities. We obtain the x-coordinate of calibration charts by computing the mean probability in each of these bins. To obtain the y-coordinate, we compute the fraction of the options that actually correspond to a correct answer within each bin. The result is a calibration chart such as Figure 6. Our method differs slightly from some others in the literature [Guo et al., 2017, Srivastava et al., 2022, Lin et al., 2022], where only the probabilities for the top prediction are included.
items.zh: 为了评估多项选择题的校准，我们获取所有答案选项（包括正确和错误选项）的概率列表。然后对这些概率进行排序，并将其分成10组，每组包含相同数量的概率。通过计算每个组内的平均概率得到校准图的x坐标。为了得到y坐标，我们计算每个组中实际对应正确答案的选项比例。结果得到如图6所示的校准图。我们的方法与文献中其他一些方法略有不同[Guo et al., 2017, Srivastava et al., 2022, Lin et al., 2022]，这些方法只包含最高预测的概率。

full.sec: A.6 Training for P(IK)

items.en: In order to train P(IK) classifiers, we created datasets that consisted of (Q𝑄Q, IK / IDK Label) pairs. Given a question Q𝑄Q, such as a TriviaQA question that includes a few-shot prompt, we generated 30 candidate answer samples at T=1𝑇1T=1. See Appendix A.7 for details on the formatting of Q𝑄Q on each of our tasks. We then scored each candidate answer sample as either correct or incorrect. For each candidate answer, we then create a datapoint (Q𝑄Q, True Label) based on whether or not the model got the answer correct. Thus, for each question, we have 30 datapoints of (Q𝑄Q, True Label). This is done as a simple way of approximating a soft label using many hard labels.
items.zh: 为了训练P(IK)分类器，我们创建了由(Q, IK/IDK标签)对组成的数据集。给定一个问题Q，例如包含少样本提示的TriviaQA问题，我们在T=1时生成30个候选答案样本。关于每个任务上Q格式的详细信息，请参见附录A.7。然后，我们将每个候选答案样本评为正确或错误。对于每个候选答案，我们根据模型是否得到正确答案创建一个数据点(Q, 真实标签)。因此，对于每个问题，我们有30个(Q, 真实标签)数据点。这是一种使用多个硬标签近似软标签的简单方法。

items.en: During fine-tuning, we used a batch size of 7680, and a learning rate that was 1313\frac{1}{3} of the original pretraining learning rate. We noticed that using a larger than normal batch size was important for learning because of our noisy soft training labels.
items.zh: 在微调期间，我们使用7680的批量大小，以及原始预训练学习率1/3的学习率。我们注意到，由于噪声软训练标签，使用比正常更大的批量大小对于学习很重要。

items.en: The evaluation datasets are constructed similarly to the above, except during evaluation, we focus on computing ground truth P(IK) scores for each question, instead of many IK/IDK labels for each question. The ground truth P(IK) is simply the fraction of T=1𝑇1T=1 samples the model gets correct. We then compare this ground truth P(IK) score to the model’s predicted P(IK) score on these evaluation questions in many plots throughout the paper.
items.zh: 评估数据集的构建与上述类似，但在评估期间，我们专注于计算每个问题的真实P(IK)分数，而不是每个问题的多个IK/IDK标签。真实P(IK)只是模型在T=1时正确样本的比例。然后，我们在论文的多个图中将这个真实P(IK)分数与模型在这些评估问题上的预测P(IK)分数进行比较。
